Japanese: 同日　午後１２時１４分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: October 14, 12:14 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃんが？
English: ...My sis?

Japanese: 聞こえたような気がしたんだよ。 ‥‥千尋さんの声が。
English: I could have sworn I heard Mia's voice.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥生きてるんだね。 なるほどくんのココロの中で。
English: So then, she's still alive... inside your heart.

Japanese: ‥‥リューイチくん！
English: ...Nicky boy!

Japanese: あ、まれかさん‥‥
English: Oh, Ms. DeLite...

Japanese: ホントなの？　あの探偵さんが 殺人犯人、なんて‥‥
English: Is it true that detective is the real killer?

Japanese: 正直なところ‥‥ 決定的な証拠は、ありません。
English: To be honest... we don't have any definite proof.

Japanese: しかし‥‥アイツ以外には 考えられないんですよ。
English: But he's the only one who could have done it.

Japanese: でも‥‥あの晩、探偵さんは 高菱屋にいたんじゃなかったの？
English: But, wasn't he at Lordly Tailor that night?

Japanese: それに、理由がわからないよ。
English: Not to mention, we don't exactly know his motive.

Japanese: どうしてアイガ探偵が、 ブスジマ社長を‥‥？
English: I mean, why would Detective Atmey want to kill Kane Bullard...?

Japanese: あ。そろそろ時間だ！ 法廷に戻らないと。
English: Oops, it's almost time! Better get back to the courtroom.

Japanese: （とにかく！　決定的な証拠を 探しながら‥‥）
English: (I need to find some solid proof...)

Japanese: （一気に攻め落とすしかない！）
English: (...and it's gotta happen sooner rather than later!)

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時２１分 地方裁判所　第６法廷
English: October 14, 12:21 PM District Court Courtroom No. 6

Japanese: それでは、 審理を再開しましょう。
English: Now then, this court is back in session.

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙さん。 ‥‥証言台へおねがいします。
English: Mr. Luke Atmey. Please take the stand.

Japanese: やあれ‥‥これはこれは弁護士殿。
English: Well well... How do you do, Sir Lawyer?

Japanese: まさか貴方ほどのカタが、 あのような暴挙に出られるとは‥‥
English: I never would have thought to see you acting so recklessly.

Japanese: 判決を下させるワケには 行かなかったんですよ。
English: I couldn't let them hand down your verdict just yet.

Japanese: ‥‥カンペキなアリバイが 成立してしまいますからね。
English: Not when it would have given you your perfect alibi.

Japanese: “怪人☆仮面マスク”という名の アリバイが‥‥。
English: An alibi by the name of "Mask☆DeMasque"...

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイ。さすがの哀牙にも、 イミがわかりませぬ。
English: I'm sorry. I'm afraid even the great Luke Atmey has no idea what you mean.

Japanese: なにぶん、今朝の１０時より今まで となりの法廷にいたものですから。
English: Of course, I have been in the next courtroom ever since 10 o'clock this morning.

Japanese: ‥‥こちらの状況など、 知るはずもないのですよ。
English: ...I'm afraid there's no way I could know what's been going on in here.

Japanese: 今日は、あなたはずっと 被告席にいたわけですな？
English: You've been in the defendant's seat all day long, correct?

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクとして 裁かれるために‥‥
English: Being tried as Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: さよう。‥‥まっこと、 世間というのはダマされやすい。
English: Indeed... It's truly child's play to fool the ignorant masses.

Japanese: 怪人たる我が正体を知らず、 警備を依頼した上‥‥
English: Not only did the poor fools ask me to "protect" their valuables...

Japanese: 気まぐれにエモノを取り返せば、 法外な依頼料までいただける始末。
English: ...they even gave me a generous reward upon returning their own property to them.

Japanese: ‥‥我が神々しき指に光る、この 赤いダイヤのリングのコト‥‥
English: Take this red diamond ring that sparkles upon my divine finger, for example...

Japanese: あくまでも、“怪人☆仮面マスクは 自分だ”と主張するつもりですか？
English: So you continue to insist that you are, in fact, "Mask☆DeMasque"?

Japanese: ‥‥言うにおよばず。
English: ...Of course.

Japanese: それでは、星威岳　哀牙さん。 まず、うかがいましょう。
English: Very well then, Luke Atmey. Let us begin with this simple question.

Japanese: ９月１２日、午前１時‥‥ 毒島　黒兵衛が殺害されました。
English: On October 12th at 1 AM, Kane Bullard was murdered.

Japanese: その時間、 あなたはどこにいましたか？
English: Where were you at that time?

Japanese: 知恵なきモノに、おのれの 無知を恥じる知恵すらなし。
English: One without knowledge lacks even the knowledge that he should be ashamed of himself.

Japanese: ‥‥ユルしてさし上げましょうぞ、 裁判長殿。
English: ...But don't worry. I will not hold it against you, Sir Judge.

Japanese: はあ、どうも‥‥。
English: Um, thanks...

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥探偵さんよ。 事件当夜のコト、話してみな。
English: Alright, Mr. Atmey... The night of the murder... Speak!

Japanese: ‥‥聞いてやるぜ！
English: ...We're listening!

Japanese: おおせのままに。‥‥検事殿。
English: As you wish... Sir Prosecutor.

Japanese: ～事件当夜のアリバイ～
English: -- The Alibi --

Japanese: ‥‥我は怪人として、予告どおり ツボめを盗んでおったのです。
English: I was stealing the urn as Mask☆DeMasque, just as I announced I would...

Japanese: 準備期間はイヤというほどあった。 たやすいシゴトでしたな。
English: I had more than enough time to prepare. It was a pathetically easy job.

Japanese: 写真は語るコトバを持たぬ‥‥だが それゆえ、いつわらざる証人です。
English: A photograph contains no words... But in this case, one turned out to be my witness.

Japanese: キャメラが怪人をとらえた時刻は、 殺人の発生時刻と一致しているッ！
English: The time at which the camera captured Mask☆DeMasque was the same time as the murder!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうやら、問題は この犯行写真、ということですな。
English: Hmm... It seems the main point of discussion will be this photo of the crime scene.

Japanese: （‥‥今までのことは、すべて アイツが仕組んだコト‥‥）
English: (Everything else up until now was all part of his plan.)

Japanese: （この写真にも‥‥かならず、 ヒミツがあるはずだ！）
English: (There has to be a secret to this picture as well!)

Japanese: ‥‥いくら仮面マスクと言えども、 その身は２つにあらず。
English: Even the great Mask☆DeMasque cannot be in two places at once.

Japanese: では、これにてシツレイ。 判決を受けねばなりませぬゆえ。
English: Now then, if you'll excuse me. I have a verdict to receive.

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥哀牙探偵。 尋問がまだです。
English: Unfortunately, Mr. Atmey, we still have to do your cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥愚者、愚者なるゆえを知らず、 ですかな。
English: ...A fool is too foolish to know that he is a fool...

Japanese: ぐしゃぐしゃだって、 なるほどくん。
English: I think he's trying to say that you're full of it, Nick.

Japanese: それは、アイツの アリバイのほうだよ！
English: The only thing that's full of it is his alibi!

Japanese: ～事件当夜のアリバイ～
English: -- The Alibi --

Japanese: ‥‥我は怪人として、予告どおり ツボめを盗んでおったのです。
English: I was stealing the urn as Mask☆DeMasque, just as I announced I would...

Japanese: 準備期間はイヤというほどあった。 たやすいシゴトでしたな。
English: I had more than enough time to prepare. It was a pathetically easy job.

Japanese: 写真は語るコトバを持たぬ‥‥だが それゆえ、いつわらざる証人です。
English: A photograph contains no words... But in this case, one turned out to be my witness.

Japanese: キャメラが怪人をとらえた時刻は、 殺人の発生時刻と一致しているッ！
English: The time at which the camera captured Mask☆DeMasque was the same time as the murder!

Japanese: このアリバイ‥‥ニセモノなの？
English: So, this alibi is false?

Japanese: そうじゃなきゃ、ＫＢ警備で 社長を殺害できないからね。
English: It has to be, or he couldn't have killed Mr. Bullard at KB Security.

Japanese: でも‥‥特にヘンなところ、 気がつかないけどな。
English: But... I'm not really spotting anything unusual.

Japanese: ‥‥可能性は、２つ。
English: There are two possibilities...

Japanese: 写真の仮面マスクがニセモノか、 写真自体がニセモノか‥‥？
English: Either the Mask☆DeMasque in the photo is a fake... Or the photo itself is.

Japanese: この写真が、その証拠ですか。
English: So this photograph is the proof, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥いかにも。
English: Indeed it is.

Japanese: 写真に写っている怪人‥‥ 仮面をつけていますね。
English: And the man in the photo is certainly wearing a mask.

Japanese: じつは、それゆえ “仮面マスク”なのですよ。
English: That is why I am called "Mask☆DeMasque".

Japanese: しかし、それゆえ‥‥この怪人の 正体がだれだか、わかりません。
English: But conveniently, that also means that there is no way to tell who this really is.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　コイツが星威岳　哀牙 じゃねえ、とでも言うつもりか？
English: Ha...! Are you saying that this is not, in fact, Luke Atmey?

Japanese: アリバイを作るため、怪人に なりすました“共犯者”‥‥
English: That it could be an accomplice dressed up as Mask☆DeMasque to create an alibi...?

Japanese: ほお‥‥おもしろいですな。
English: Oh ho... What an interesting idea.

Japanese: このロンリイ・ウルフの哀牙に 共犯者がいた、と？
English: Are you saying that I, Lone Wolf Luke, had an accomplice?

Japanese: （殺人のあった時間‥‥哀牙探偵が ＫＢ警備にいたのならば‥‥）
English: (If Luke Atmey was at KB Security during the murder...)

Japanese: （この写真の仮面マスクは ニセモノのはずだ‥‥）
English: (...then the Mask☆DeMasque in this picture has to be a fake.)

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。 あなたには、共犯者がいた！
English: ...Exactly. You had an accomplice!

Japanese: 言っちゃったな？　まるほどう。 ‥‥もう、後にはひけねえなあ。
English: Now remember you've said that, Mr. Trite, because there's no going back.

Japanese: コンキョのない言いがかりなら、 オレからペナルティをオゴるぜ！
English: If you're making baseless claims, I'll treat you to a penalty myself!

Japanese: 煮えたぎってるよ‥‥ あのペナルティ。
English: That penalty is probably boiling hot, too...

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、ダレだ？ その“共犯者”ってのは！
English: So who is it, Mr. Trite!? Who was the accomplice!?

Japanese: ええと‥‥ このヒトです。共犯者は。
English: Umm... It was this person.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ とんだイタズラっ子だぜ‥‥。
English: Ha...! That's quite the prank you're trying to pull...

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥あるのかい？ ソイツが共犯者だ、っていう。
English: Do you have any evidence that this person is, in fact, the accomplice?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well...

Japanese: く‥‥空気です！　この人物が かもし出している空気が‥‥
English: The... The aura! The aura that this person has around them is...

Japanese: 空気で判断するなら‥‥
English: If you're going to judge someone by their aura...

Japanese: アヤシイのは あなた自身ですぞ、弁護人！
English: ...you'd be right up there as the number one suspect, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （うう‥‥ 勝負を急ぎすぎたか‥‥！）
English: (Nnrgh... I jumped the gun on that one!)

Japanese: （殺人のあった時間‥‥哀牙探偵が ＫＢ警備にいたのならば‥‥）
English: (If Luke Atmey was at KB Security during the murder...)

Japanese: （この写真の仮面マスクは ニセモノのはずだ‥‥）
English: (...then the Mask☆DeMasque in this picture has to be a fake.)

Japanese: じゃあ！　やっぱり、 共犯者がいたんだよ！
English: Then there really was an accomplice!

Japanese: でも‥‥今のぼくには、 それがダレだか、わからないんだ。
English: But right now, I have no idea who it was.

Japanese: うう‥‥今のあたしにも それはわからないケドさ。
English: Hmm... I don't have any idea right now either.

Japanese: （コンキョのない異議は 相手の思うツボだ‥‥）
English: (Baseless objections are just what that guy wants...)

Japanese: （他に道があるかもしれない ‥‥じっくり行こう！）
English: (There's got to be another way... And I'm going to find it!)

Japanese: たしか、あなたが霧緒さんから 警備の依頼を受けたのは‥‥
English: I believe Adrian Andrews hired you at one point?

Japanese: さよう‥‥今から２０日以上 前のコトでしたかな。
English: That's right... That was over 20 days ago, if I'm not mistaken.

Japanese: 高菱屋に予告状が届いたのが、 事件の１０日ほど前ですから‥‥
English: You sent the calling card to Lordly Tailor 10 days before the heist...

Japanese: あなたは、警備を始めた後‥‥ 予告状を出したコトになりますね？
English: That would mean you sent out the card after you began your security watch, right?

Japanese: さよう。せっかく警備のシゴトを やっているのですからな。
English: Indeed. There was no reason why I couldn't do both jobs at once.

Japanese: エモノをいただくには 絶好のチャンスだったのですよ。
English: It was the perfect opportunity to steal my latest target.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。 悪質ですねえ、つくづく。
English: I see... You truly are evil, aren't you?

Japanese: さよう。悪質なのですよ‥‥。
English: Yes. Evil is what I am...

Japanese: ‥‥ね。なるほどくん。 ちょっとヘンじゃない？
English: Hey, Nick... Isn't there something odd about this?

Japanese: ？　どうしたの？
English: Hm?

Japanese: ‥‥アイガ探偵って、 “名探偵ぶり”がジマンなのにさ。
English: Detective Atmey was always proud of his "Ace Detective" skills, right?

Japanese: 自分ヒトリで警備していた 高菱屋からエモノを盗まれたら‥‥
English: But if the urn was stolen from Lordly Tailor while he was the only one watching it...

Japanese: 名探偵どころか、 イイワケしようがないよ。
English: ...he'd have no way to maintain his perfect "Ace Detective" persona.

Japanese: （たしかに。言われてみれば、 ちょっと不自然かな‥‥）
English: (You know, that's true... It is kind of odd.)

Japanese: “写真”‥‥というのは、 もちろん、この証拠写真ですね？
English: So by "photograph", you mean this piece of evidence here. Is that correct?

Japanese: さようッ。これこそ！　我が犯行を 立証する、決定的な証拠ですな。
English: Indeed it is. That is it! The very thing that proves I committed the crime.

Japanese: 決定的な‥‥証拠‥‥
English: The very thing... that proves you committed the crime...

Japanese: （そもそも‥‥それが オカシイんだよな‥‥）
English: (When you think about it... It's really odd...)

Japanese: ‥‥まるで、ワザとこの写真を 撮影したような言いかたですね‥‥
English: You say that almost as if you had this picture taken on purpose...

Japanese: 自分がしかけた防犯カメラの ワナに、自分が引っかかる‥‥
English: He was simply caught by the very camera that he had set up...

Japanese: そんな日だってあるさ。
English: We all have days like that.

Japanese: さよう。‥‥完全犯罪など しょせんは見果てぬユメ。
English: Indeed... It turned out that there was no such thing as the perfect crime after all.

Japanese: 人生とは、かくもヒニクなもの。 ブルウでメロウなるメロデエ‥‥
English: Life is truly an ironic thing. A sad, blue melody...

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、もう少し 情報を集めたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I'd better gather more information for now...)

Japanese: （ヤツが殺人事件の真犯人なら‥‥ 写真には、かならずウソがある）
English: (If he's truly the killer... there's got to be something phony in that photo.)

Japanese: “写真”‥‥というのは、 もちろん、この証拠写真ですね？
English: So by "photograph", you mean this piece of evidence here. Is that correct?

Japanese: さよう。これこそ！　我が犯行を 立証する、決定的な証拠ですな。
English: Indeed it is. That is it! The very thing that proves I committed the crime.

Japanese: 決定的な‥‥証拠‥‥
English: The very thing... that proves you committed the crime...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、アンタにも わかってるハズだぜ‥‥。
English: Surely even you understand by now.

Japanese: 防犯キャメラは高菱屋が用意した。 ゆえに、写真に細工の余地はなし。
English: Lordly Tailor provided that camera. There's no way I could have tampered with it.

Japanese: 共犯者がいないかぎり、殺害は 不可能‥‥ではないですかな？
English: That means I could not have killed Kane Bullard... unless I had an accomplice.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmmm...

Japanese: （‥‥事件当夜の状況を、 よく考えるんだ‥‥）
English: (Come on, think long and hard about that night...)

Japanese: （あの晩の地下倉庫と、それを 写したという、この写真‥‥）
English: (The basement warehouse, and this picture that supposedly captures it...)

Japanese: （そもそも‥‥“不自然な点”が 存在しないだろうか‥‥？）
English: (It's got to be here... Isn't there something "funny" about this picture?)

Japanese: この写真が‥‥ニセモノだと？
English: Are you implying that this picture is a fake?

Japanese: ‥‥少なくとも、不自然な点が あるのはジジツです。
English: You bet I am. There's definitely something strange about this picture.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥ぼくたちは、事件が 起こる前に、地下倉庫を見ました。
English: We took a look around the basement warehouse that night, before the theft took place.

Japanese: そのときのキオクと食いちがう 奇妙な点が、この写真にあります！
English: And there's something in this photo that doesn't match my memory of that night!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人の 考えをうかがいましょう！
English: ...Very well, then let's hear what you have to say!

Japanese: この写真‥‥現場の状況の、 どこが不自然だというのですか！
English: What about this photograph do you find "funny"?

Japanese: アヤシイのは、この部分です！
English: This part here!

Japanese: それは、“日時”‥‥ですか？
English: You mean the "date stamp"...?

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥これは、 “日時”とは呼べません！
English: Unfortunately... I'm afraid you can't call this a "date stamp"!

Japanese: ‥‥カンジンな“日付” がありませんからね。
English: ...Because there is no sign of a "date" here at all!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥それは “不自然な点”とは呼べねえぜ。
English: Unfortunately, I'm afraid you can't call that "funny" either, Mr. Trite!
Huh...?

Japanese: たしかに、“撮影時刻”だけを 記録する防犯カメラは不完全だ。
English: While a security camera that only records the time leaves something to be desired...

Japanese: だが‥‥それを用意したのは 高菱屋百貨店のスタッフだ。
English: ...the Lordly Tailor staff are the ones who set up the camera.

Japanese: ‥‥そこの証人の セキニンじゃねえからな。
English: It is not the fault of this witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ それは、そうかもしれませんな。
English: Hmm... That's certainly true.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
（もっとベツの見かたを したほうがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I need to approach this from a different angle...)

Japanese: それでは、証人！　‥‥証言に 戻っていただきましょう。
English: Very well then, witness! Let's return to your testimony.

Japanese: 不自然なのは‥‥ このへんではないでしょうか！
English: Perhaps THIS is the area in question!

Japanese: ‥‥“そのへん”、ですか‥‥
English: This "area"...? Could you be any more vague?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　やるせねえぜ、 今日のコーヒーは‥‥。
English: Ha...! Today's java has a little extra kick to it...

Japanese: どうやら、不自然なのは‥‥
English: It seems that the funny part...

Japanese: 弁護席あたりだった、 というコトでしょうな！
English: ...lies in the "area" of the defense!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！　もっと キチンと考えないと‥‥！）
English: (Ouch... I better think this through, or I'm gonna fall victim to another drubbing.)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言に戻っていただきましょう！
English: Very well then, witness! Let's return to your testimony.

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ 特にモンダイはありません。
English: Unfortunately... I don't see anything wrong.

Japanese: それはザンネンでしたね。
English: That is indeed unfortunate.

Japanese: ‥‥オトコはいつも、 スッキリしない生き物なのさ。
English: Men, as a species, are doomed to a life of bitterness and frustration.

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。 あたし、思うんだけど‥‥
English: Hey, Nick. I've been thinking...

Japanese: ‥‥？　どうしたの、真宵ちゃん。
English: What is it, Maya?

Japanese: これに写っている現場って、 あの晩の地下倉庫だよね。
English: This photo was taken in that basement warehouse, right?

Japanese: あたしたちが《秘宝展》を 見に行った夜の‥‥。
English: And if this was taken on the same night as when we went to see the Treasure Exhibit...

Japanese: もし、そうなら‥‥１カ所、 ヘンなところがないかなあ。
English: ...then there's one thing here that doesn't add up.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ベツの見かたを したほうがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I need to approach this from a different angle...)

Japanese: 現場の不自然な点‥‥ それは、ここです！
English: The funny part... is right here!

Japanese: こ‥‥これは、なんと‥‥ 《血痕》ではないですかッ！
English: Why, this... this is a bloodstain!

Japanese: むむ！　事件は、がぜん 血ナマぐさくなってきましたな！
English: Ah! Blood! Now this case is getting interesting!

Japanese: ‥‥い、いえいえ。そのシミは ピンクのペンキです。
English: Umm, not exactly... This stain is actually pink paint.

Japanese: なんだ‥‥ただのペイントですか。 しかも、もも色の。
English: Oh, just paint... And peach-colored at that.

Japanese: （ロコツにガッカリされた）
English: (From blood to peaches... The judge sure loves going on his wild tangents.)

Japanese: 問題は、写真に写っている ペンキではないのです。
English: The problem with this photograph is not the paint.

Japanese: あの晩の地下倉庫の状況を 考えたら‥‥
English: The problem is, when you consider the layout of the basement warehouse...

Japanese: 本来、写っているべき “あるもの”が存在していない！
English: It turns out that "something" that should be there is nowhere to be seen!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、うかがいましょう。
English: ...Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: このペイントのかわりに‥‥何が 写っているべきだと言うのですか？
English: What is supposed to be in this picture instead of the paint stains?

Japanese: 今の証拠は なんですかな？　弁護人。
English: So what is the evidence this time, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あ、その‥‥とりあえず、 イキオイで投げつけてみました。
English: Um, well... I thought I'd just kind of throw this out and see...

Japanese: 私はゴミ捨て場じゃ ありませんぞ！
English: I'll have you know I am not a trashcan!

Japanese: （あと一歩だったのに‥‥）
English: (And I was so close, too...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、時間のムダ だったようですな！
English: It seems you've just been wasting our time!

Japanese: めずらしいコトじゃねえぜ。 ‥‥このセンセイの場合‥‥
English: That's nothing unusual for this guy...

Japanese: それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well then, witness. Proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥あいや。
English: ...Certainly.

Japanese: その写真を撮影した 防犯カメラのコトですけど‥‥
English: About the camera that took this photograph...

Japanese: やあれ！　あなたの考えている コトは、わかりますぞ！
English: Oh come now! It's all too clear what you're thinking!
...Huh?

Japanese: おおかた、写真の時刻に細工した ‥‥そう考えているのでしょう。
English: You think I altered the timestamp on the photograph, don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、あり得ねえぜ。
English: I'm afraid that's impossible.

Japanese: ‥‥防犯カメラは、高菱屋が 用意したモノだ。さらに‥‥
English: The camera was set up by Lordly Tailor. And on top of that...

Japanese: 写真のデータをプリントしたのも、 高菱屋が用意したスタッフだ。
English: ...it was Lordly Tailor staff that printed that picture's data.

Japanese: ザンネンだが‥‥ 写真に、細工の余地はねえ。
English: Unfortunately for the defense, there's no way that picture could have been altered.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: （‥‥他に、アヤシイ点を 探したほうがいいか‥‥）
English: (It looks like I'd better find something else that could be suspicious...)

Japanese: 《倉院の里・秘宝展》の セキニンシャは、こう言ってます。
English: The supervisor of the Treasure Exhibit stated the following.

Japanese: ちょうど、あの日の お昼ごろのコトだったんです。
English: Well, there's a good reason for that. On the day of the crime, around noon...

Japanese: 山奥の霊場から、あの黄金像が 倉庫に届いたのは‥‥。
English: ...that golden statue just happened to arrive from the mountain training hall.

Japanese: あの黄金像‥‥ペンキの跡をかくす のに、ちょうどいい大きさでした。
English: I realized that the statue would be the perfect size for covering up the paint stains.

Japanese: だから‥‥私、あの場所へ 置いておいたんです。
English: That's why... I put it where you first saw it.

Japanese: ぼくはジッサイ、盗難が起こる前の 倉庫で、黄金像を目撃しています。
English: I myself was there the night the theft took place, and saw the statue in that spot.

Japanese: あの晩、倉庫の写真を撮れば‥‥ 黄金像が写っているはずなのです！
English: If this picture was truly taken on that night, then that statue should've been there!

Japanese: しかし、事件の翌日。 オレが現場に行ったときには‥‥
English: But when I went there the day after the theft...

Japanese: あのバアさんの像は、 部屋のスミで小さくなってたぜ。
English: ...that statue of the old bag was sitting in the corner.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。何かのはずみで 動いてしまったのでしょうか‥‥？
English: Hmmm... Perhaps it was somehow pushed there accidentally...?

Japanese: 黄金像は、裁判長よりも やや大きなシロモノです。
English: Your Honor, this statue is slightly larger than yourself and quite heavy.

Japanese: 理由もなく、動くはずがない！
English: It would take more than an accidental push to move it that distance!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　それなら‥‥アンタは 立証できるっていうのかい？
English: Ha...! In that case... Can you prove it?

Japanese: “ダレが”“なんのために”‥‥ あの晩、黄金像を動かしたのか！
English: Can you give us the "rhyme and reason" as to why that statue was moved that night?

Japanese: り、立証できるっていうの？ なるほどくん！
English: C-Can you do it, Nick?

Japanese: （像を動かした人物の正体は ともかく‥‥）
English: (Never mind who moved it...)

Japanese: （問題は、“なぜ動かしたか” ‥‥その理由だな）
English: (The real question is "why did they move it"...)

Japanese: それでは‥‥ うかがいましょう。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. I hope you are prepared with your answer.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥黄金像を動かした 人物は、だれだったのですか？
English: Now then, who was the one that moved the golden statue on the night of the crime?

Japanese: いかがですか？　ゴドー検事。
English: What do you think, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトは、自分の問題には 自分でコタエを出すモンだぜ。
English: A man should be able to answer his own questions. That's what I think.

Japanese: オレは今、オレ自身の ２つの問題で精いっぱいさ。
English: Right now I'm busy trying to come up with two answers of my own.

Japanese: コーヒーはなぜ、黒いのか？ そして‥‥なぜ、ニガいのか‥‥？
English: Why is coffee black? And... Why is it bitter...?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。手みじかに言うと、 ハズレ、ということですな。
English: Mr. Wright... To put it simply, you've messed up.

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん！
English: What are you doing, Nick!?

Japanese: どうして像を動かしたか、 その理由も考えないと！
English: You need to think about why that statue was moved!

Japanese: （こうしてみんなが怒っている あいだに、もう一度考えよう！）
English: (The good thing about when everyone chews me out is that it gives me time to think!)

Japanese: 像を動かしたのは‥‥哀牙探偵！ もちろん、あなたしかいない！
English: The one who moved the statue is none other than... Luke Atmey!

Japanese: ‥‥あいや、弁護士殿！ また、珍にして妙なるコトを‥‥
English: Come now, Sir Lawyer! There you go again on one of your strange delusions...

Japanese: 弁護人！　その珍にして妙なる 自信のコンキョはなんですか！
English: Mr. Wright! What basis do you have for your strange delusions!?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。
English: ...It's very simple.

Japanese: あの晩、地下倉庫にいたのは‥‥ この証人だけでしたからね。
English: The witness was the only one in the basement warehouse that night.

Japanese: たしかに、シムプルですな。 ‥‥んがッ！　しかしッ！
English: That is indeed very simple... However!

Japanese: なぜこの我が、重い黄金像を 動かさねばならぬのですかな？
English: Why would I want to move a heavy golden statue?

Japanese: （黄金像を動かした“理由”。 ‥‥ここからが勝負だな‥‥）
English: (The reason for moving the golden statue... Here's where our battle really begins.)

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: どんな理由があって、この証人が 黄金像を動かしたのですか！
English: What reason did the witness have to move that statue!?

Japanese: その“理由”は‥‥ おそらく、この写真にあります。
English: The reason can be found here in this photograph.

Japanese: 哀牙探偵。あなたが仮面マスクに なりすましたのは‥‥
English: Luke Atmey. You pretended to be Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: “あの晩、高菱屋にいた”という アリバイを作るためだった。
English: ...to create an alibi by showing you were "at Lordly Tailor that night".

Japanese: この写真の中には、１つだけ ‥‥大きなウソがある。
English: But this photograph contains a single, fatal flaw.

Japanese: もし、黄金像が写っていたら、 そのウソが成立しなくなる‥‥
English: If the statue had been there, your lie would be exposed like cheap film at a drug store...

Japanese: だからこそ、 あなたは像を動かしたのです！
English: That is why you moved the statue!

Japanese: 写真の中に‥‥“ウソ”！
English: A single, fatal flaw?

Japanese: おもしれえ‥‥ ぜひ、教えてほしいモンだぜ。
English: Interesting theory... Please enlighten us.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、この写真のどこに、 “ウソ”があるのか！
English: Just where in this picture does this "lie" exist!?

Japanese: ウソは‥‥この 怪人☆仮面マスクです！
English: The lie... is Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: な、なんと‥‥この怪人が ニセモノだというのですか！
English: A-Are you saying that this Mask☆DeMasque is a fake!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
アンタ‥‥自分で言ったコトに セキニンを持ってほしいな‥‥
English: You know... I really wish you would start acting more responsibly...

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...Huh?

Japanese: 今の問題は、“黄金像が 動かされた理由”だぜ。
English: The current question is, "why was the golden statue moved?"

Japanese: その理由と、怪人の正体 ‥‥なんのカンケイがあるんだ？
English: So what possible connection could there be with Mask☆DeMasque's identity?

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった！）
English: (Oh buggers!)

Japanese: 今の“し‥‥しまった！”と 言いたげなカオに、
English: Your face has "Oh buggers!" written all over it.

Japanese: ペナルティを与えます！
English: That's a penalty for you!

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、聞くぜ。 まるほどう。
English: I'll ask you again, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: 写真のウソは、 このあたりにありますッ！
English: The lie in this photo is somewhere around here!

Japanese: おっと‥‥アンタこそ、 ウソはいけねえぜ。
English: Jumping to conclusions again? I think you need to take a gander in your local mirror.

Japanese: ウソを申すは、つねに コトバを持つ人間のみ。
English: As the Yiddish proverb goes, "A half-truth is a whole lie".

Japanese: ‥‥ウソつきは ふり返りざまに有罪ですぞ。
English: Keep lying like this and you'll be the one on trial.

Japanese: （‥‥よってたかって ウソつきにされてしまった）
English: (Oh man, now they're calling me a liar...!)

Japanese: 問題は‥‥供子さまの像が なぜ動かされたか、だよね？
English: The question is why was the statue moved, right?

Japanese: この像が写真に写っていては ツゴウが悪かった‥‥
English: There was some reason the culprit didn't want the statue in the picture...

Japanese: ‥‥そう思ったんだけどな。
English: At least, that's what I thought.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、聞くぜ。 まるほどう。
English: I'll ask you again, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥この写真のウソは、 この“撮影時刻”です。
English: Naturally... the lie in this photo is the timestamp!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: 弁護側の主張は、ただひとつ！
English: I'll tell you exactly what I mean!

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙はあの晩、ＫＢ警備で ブスジマ社長を殺害した。
English: On the night in question, Luke Atmey went to KB Security and murdered Kane Bullard!

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥当然！
English: Therefore, it's obvious!

Japanese: 事件当夜、この時刻！　証人が 高菱屋にいたはずがないッ！
English: It would have been impossible for him to have been at Lordly Tailor at this time!

Japanese: しかし！　それと、 黄金像が動かされた理由は‥‥
English: But what does that have to do with the statue being moved...?

Japanese: 裁判長。思い出してください。
English: Remember if you will, Your Honor.

Japanese: この像が‥‥“いつ”、倉庫の 扉の横に置かれたのか‥‥？
English: When was this statue placed besides the warehouse door...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well...

Japanese: ‥‥黄金像は、事件の当日、 あの地下倉庫に運びこまれた。
English: The statue was taken down to the warehouse on the day of the crime...

Japanese: そして、ペンキの跡を かくすため、あそこに置かれた‥‥
English: ...and it was placed there in order to cover up the paint.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: ...Exactly.

Japanese: 哀牙探偵は、被害者を殺害する 時刻を、あらかじめ決めていた。
English: Luke Atmey had already decided on the time when he was going to kill the victim.

Japanese: そして、その時間のアリバイを 作るために‥‥
English: And so, in order to create an alibi for that time...

Japanese: 事件の起こる何日も前に、 この写真を撮影しておいたのです！
English: ...he took this picture days before the murder took place!

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: Wh-What the...!?

Japanese: ‥‥当然、黄金像はまだ、 地下倉庫に運びこまれていません。
English: Of course, the statue hadn't yet been brought down to the basement warehouse yet.
Ah...

Japanese: そして、事件当日‥‥
English: So, on the day of the crime, Mr. Atmey must have been quite nervous!

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙探偵は、 かなりアセったはずです。
English: As nervous as a long-tailed cat in a rocking chair factory, so to speak.

Japanese: あってはならない場所に、 あってはならないモノが‥‥
English: Why? Because something that wasn't supposed to be there...

Japanese: 運びこまれて しまったのですから‥‥。
English: ...had been brought down and placed where it wasn't supposed to be.

Japanese: だからこそ！
English: And that...

Japanese: 哀牙探偵は、事件当夜‥‥ 像を動かさなければならなかった！
English: ...is why Luke Atmey had to move the statue on the night of the murder!

Japanese: あらかじめ撮影しておいた、 この写真に合わせるために！
English: He did it to make the room match with the way it had been in his photo!

Japanese: ぐふほほほおおッ！
English: GWAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！ 哀牙さん！　そうなのですか！
English: Order! Order! Mr. Atmey! Is this true!?

Japanese: ま、待たれよ、ご老人！ こ、これをお忘れですかな？
English: One moment, Your Honor! H-Have you forgotten this?
Wh-What's that...?

Japanese: 地下倉庫のコムピュウタ ‥‥そのデエタでござる！
English: The data for the basement warehouse computer!

Japanese: これによると‥‥事件当夜、 キャメラは作動しておりますぞ！
English: According to this, the camera did indeed go off on the night of the crime!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥防犯キャメラは 高菱屋が用意したモノでした。
English: Hmm... It's true that the camera had been set up by the Lordly Tailor staff.

Japanese: しかし！　そのデエタを管理した プログラムは、あなたのものだ！
English: However! The program used to manage that data was yours!

Japanese: そこには、細工の余地が あったハズです！
English: That alone would have allowed you to tamper with the data!
Ah...

Japanese: あいやああああァァァァッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAHHH!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　ゴドー検事！ これはいったい‥‥
English: Order! Order! Mr. Godot! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ここ、コラ！ コーヒーを飲まない！
English: Godot! I warned you about making me wait! Now put that coffee down!
...My 11th cup.

Japanese: オレは、裁判中のコーヒーは １７杯まで、と決めている。
English: I've promised to drink no more than 17 during a trial.

Japanese: まだ６杯‥‥よゆうがあるぜ。
English: Which means... I'm still good 'til the last drop.

Japanese: しかし、弁護人の主張には、 たしかに一理ありますぞ！
English: However, the defense has a very good point!

Japanese: “一理”だ‥‥？ それぐらい、なんだってんだ？
English: "A good point"...? So what?

Japanese: オレたちは‥‥、万里の道を行く 旅人なんだぜ‥‥。
English: We are all but travelers on a road of infinite points.

Japanese: “一理”と“万里”って‥‥ 単位がちがうんだけど。
English: Um, I think you've got your "points" mixed up with your other "points"...

Japanese: ‥‥この写真は、 前もって撮影されていた。
English: ...So you say this photograph was taken ahead of time.

Japanese: そして、写真に合わせるために 黄金像は動かされた‥‥
English: And that the statue was moved in order to make it match...

Japanese: なかなかおもしろいぜ。 ただ‥‥
English: That's a very interesting idea. However.

Japanese: １つだけ、笑えねえ点がある。
English: There's one point that can't be denied.
Which is...?

Japanese: ただの“可能性”だってコトさ。
English: That it's only... a "possibility".

Japanese: “かもしれない”にシバられ ちまったオトコは‥‥
English: Men that are trapped by the chains of "maybe"...

Japanese: でっかいユメ、追えねえぜ！
English: ...can never reach their dreams!

Japanese: た、たしかに！
English: Th-That's very true!

Japanese: いやいや！　そこで ナットクしないでください！
English: N-No way! Don't fall for that, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥オイ！　怪人さんよォ！
English: Hey! Mr. DeMasque!

Japanese: あ、あいやッ！
English: Y-Yes!?

Japanese: 仮面マスクとしての、アンタの行動 ‥‥それにムジュンがなければ、
English: If there's no funny business in your actions as Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: 何もモンダイはねえさ。 ‥‥話してみな。
English: ...there should be no problem with you telling us your strategy. So let's hear it.

Japanese: それでは、証言をして いただきましょう。
English: Yes. Please provide this court with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥《倉院のツボ》の 窃盗計画について‥‥！
English: About your plan to steal the Sacred Urn!

Japanese: ～倉院のツボ・窃盗計画～
English: -- The Sacred Urn Heist --

Japanese: 哀牙が高菱屋より警備の依頼を 受けたのは、今から２０日ほど前。
English: I first received the request from Lordly Tailor about 20 days ago.

Japanese: ツボはハコに入れられ、ズヴァリ！ 地下倉庫に管理されておりました。
English: The urn was placed in a box, and Zvarri! It was then sent to the warehouse.

Japanese: だから犯行当日まで、ジツブツを 見ることはできませんでしたが‥‥
English: Hence, I was actually unable to see the urn for myself until the day of the crime...

Japanese: 貴重な秘宝と聞いていたので １０日ほど前。予告状をポストに！
English: I knew it was an extremely valuable treasure, so I sent my card 10 days beforehand.

Japanese: ‥‥安全な犯行にそなえて 警備は哀牙ひとりで行ったのです！
English: I then handled security by myself to ensure that my crime would go smoothly!

Japanese: そして、１２日・午前１時！ 我は初めて、ツボを手にしたッ！
English: At last, I held the urn in my hands for the first time at 1 AM on October 12th.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、聞いたことがある 情報が多いね。
English: That's pretty much all stuff we've heard before, isn't it?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥そうじゃないトコロに 手がかりがあるんじゃないかな。
English: Yeah, but we will find the truth hidden in the nuggets of new information he gave.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥今度こそ、 まちがいはありませんな？
English: Witness... You're sure there are no mistakes this time?
...Zvarri.

Japanese: それでは！ 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Very well then! Mr. Wright! You may begin your cross-examination!

Japanese: ～倉院のツボ・窃盗計画～
English: -- The Sacred Urn Heist --

Japanese: 哀牙が高菱屋より警備の依頼を 受けたのは、今から２０日ほど前。
English: I first received the request from Lordly Tailor about 20 days ago.

Japanese: ツボはハコに入れられ、ズヴァリ！ 地下倉庫に管理されておりました。
English: The urn was placed in a box, and Zvarri! It was then sent to the warehouse.

Japanese: だから犯行当日まで、ジツブツを 見ることはできませんでしたが‥‥
English: Hence, I was actually unable to see the urn for myself until the day of the crime...

Japanese: 貴重な秘宝と聞いていたので １０日ほど前。予告状をポストに！
English: I knew it was an extremely valuable treasure, so I sent my card 10 days beforehand.

Japanese: ‥‥安全な犯行にそなえて 警備は哀牙ひとりで行ったのです！
English: I then handled security by myself to ensure that my crime would go smoothly!

Japanese: そして、１２日・午前１時！ 我は初めて、ツボを手にしたッ！
English: At last, I held the urn in my hands for the first time at 1 AM on October 12th.

Japanese: ううん‥‥ ちょっと少ないねー、情報。
English: Hmmm... Not a lot to go on, is there?

Japanese: 用心してるんだろうね、 あの探偵も‥‥。
English: That detective's been covering his tracks...

Japanese: （この尋問で証明するのは、 ただ１つ‥‥！）
English: There's only one thing we need to prove in this cross-examination...!

Japanese: （“哀牙は、事件当夜より ずっと前にこの写真を撮った”）
English: That he took this photo well before the actual night of the crime.

Japanese: そのためには‥‥ どうすればいいのかな？
English: Alright then... How do we do that?

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙探偵、さっきから しきりに主張してるよね。
English: There's one thing that Atmey stated very clearly.

Japanese: “犯行当日まで、 ツボを見たことがない”って‥‥
English: That he had never seen the urn before he stole it...

Japanese: それが“ウソだ”って 立証できればいいんだけどねえ‥‥
English: We need to prove that he's lying...

Japanese: 依頼したのは、 華宮　霧緒さんですね？
English: You were asked to guard the urn by Ms. Adrian Andrews, is that right?

Japanese: さよう‥‥我がズヴァ抜けた 評判を聞きつけたようです。
English: Indeed... It seemed that they had heard of my Zvarrkling reputation.

Japanese: （霧緒さん‥‥あいかわらず ヘンなオトコに引っかかるな）
English: (I see Ms. Andrews is still attracting the weirdos.)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも 気をつけるんだぞ。
English: You'd better watch out too, Maya.

Japanese: このハコ‥‥ですか？
English: You mean... this box?

Japanese: そう。そのソマツなハコです。
English: Yes. That pathetic box.

Japanese: キリオ嬢は、かなりそのツボを ダイジにしていて‥‥
English: Lady Andrews was especially taken with that urn...

Japanese: この哀牙すら、地下倉庫内に 立ち入ることをユルさなかった。
English: ...and she wouldn't let anyone enter the storeroom, not even me.

Japanese: （‥‥それは、初めて聞くな‥‥）
English: (Huh. Interesting new tidbit...)

Japanese: “ツボを見たことがない”‥‥ まちがいありませんか？
English: So you're sure that you had never seen the urn before?

Japanese: さよう‥‥ キリオ嬢に聞いてみればよい。
English: Indeed... You may ask Lady Andrews to confirm for yourself.

Japanese: 我が警備は、あの地下倉庫への 入り口に集中していたのです。
English: My security was focused entirely on the entrance to that warehouse.

Japanese: ただし！　この哀牙、情報だけは 見逃さず、聞き逃さず！
English: However! I, Luke Atmey, let no information whatsoever slip through my fingers!

Japanese: この予告状‥‥ですね？
English: You mean... this calling card?

Japanese: さあれ！　我がいとし子‥‥ 予告状に、まちがいないですな。
English: That's right! That is, without a doubt, my calling card.

Japanese: やっぱり、これ‥‥優作くんが 作ったんじゃなかったのかな。
English: I guess Mr. DeLite didn't make this after all.

Japanese: （哀牙探偵‥‥エンブレムの ことは、モチロン知っていた）
English: (Of course... Because Atmey knew about the emblem.)

Japanese: （予告状を作るのは カンタンだっただろうな‥‥）
English: (Making this card authentic would've been child's play to him.)

Japanese: では、地下倉庫には ダレも入らなかったんですか？
English: Then no one entered the basement warehouse?

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥秘宝が 次つぎに運びこまれてきたゆえ。
English: Unfortunately... There were many different treasures being taken there.

Japanese: 事件当日をさかのぼる ５日ほどのあいだは‥‥、
English: Thus, for a period of approximately 5 days...

Japanese: ひっきりなしに ヒトが出入りしておりましたな。
English: ...people were indeed going in and out of the warehouse.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmmm...

Japanese: そのときの写真が、コレ ‥‥というワケですか。
English: And this is the photo that proves that, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。たとえ写真を 撮られようと‥‥
English: ...Indeed. To be honest, even if a photo was taken...

Japanese: 我に不利になるとは 思わなかったのですよ。
English: I didn't think it would matter terribly much.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。 この写真がニセモノなら‥‥、
English: Hey, Nick. If this photo is a fake...

Japanese: アイガ探偵、このときに ツボを盗んだのかもしれないね。
English: Detective Atmey might have stolen the urn whenever this was taken.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかになあ。
English: ...That's true.

Japanese: アイツ、『事件当夜に初めて ツボを見た』って言ってるから‥‥
English: You know, he's been saying that he "never saw the urn until that night"...

Japanese: そこにムジュンがあれば、 イッパツなんだけどなあ。
English: If we can prove that he's lying there, we can wrap this up and put a bow on it.

Japanese: （もっと、よく見てみるか‥‥ 法廷記録を‥‥！）
English: (I need to take a good hard look at the Court Record!)

Japanese: 哀牙さん。‥‥もし、あなたが 怪人☆仮面マスクならば‥‥
English: Mr. Atmey... If you really are Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: この予告状も書いた。 ‥‥まちがいありませんね？
English: ...then you also wrote this calling card, correct?

Japanese: しごく当然‥‥でしょうな。
English: But of course.

Japanese: その予告状‥‥ 何か、問題でもあるのかい？
English: Is there a problem with that calling card?

Japanese: ‥‥予告状をよく読むと、 こう書いてあります。
English: Allow me to read a passage from the calling card that Mask☆DeMasque had written.

Japanese: 『まだらのツボには、ご用心を』 “まだら”というのは‥‥
English: "Take good care of the speckled urn." Now, the "speckled" here...

Japanese: もちろん、このピンクの模様を さすのでしょう。
English: ...surely refers to this pink pattern on the Sacred Urn.

Japanese: それはそうでしょうが‥‥ 何か、モンダイでも？
English: Yes, that is true... But so what?

Japanese: ‥‥仮面マスクが、この模様を 知っていたハズがないんですよ。
English: Truth be told, there is no way that Mask☆DeMasque could have known about this pattern.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト、ですかな‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: このツボのまだら模様は、 じつは‥‥ペンキのシミなのです。
English: This pink, spotted pattern on the urn... is actually nothing more then paint stains.

Japanese: ペイントの‥‥？
English: Paint stains...?

Japanese: そして、そのシミがついたのは‥‥
English: And these stains did not appear...

Japanese: 高菱屋百貨店に運びこまれた “後”のことでした！
English: ...until after the urn had been taken to Lordly Tailor!
Hhhmph...!

Japanese: ‥‥笑えねえジョークだぜ‥‥！
English: I'm not finding this joke to be very funny, Mr. Trite!

Japanese: 倉院のツボが、 地下倉庫に搬入された日。
English: The day that the Sacred Urn was taken to the warehouse...

Japanese: ‥‥ある人物の不注意で、 ツボは割れてしまった‥‥
English: ...the urn was broken due to human error, or should I say an error-prone human.

Japanese: ピンクのペンキは、 そのときに付着したものなのです！
English: And that's when the pink paint got on the urn!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ バ‥‥バカな‥‥ッ！
English: Urghh...! Y-You can't be serious...!

Japanese: しかし‥‥この“予告状”には、 ハッキリ模様のことが書いてある。
English: And yet this "calling card" clearly mentions the paint pattern.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: Which means...

Japanese: 哀牙探偵は、事件発生より以前に このツボを見ていた！
English: Detective Atmey had seen this urn long before the crime ever took place!

Japanese: そう！　‥‥このニセ写真を デッチ上げたときに！
English: In fact, he saw it when this fake photo was taken!

Japanese: ‥‥ぶほほおおおおッ！
English: ...Phwoooooh!

Japanese: そして、この写真が ニセモノだった以上！
English: And because this photo is a fake...

Japanese: あなたの事件当夜のアリバイは、 なくなってしまうのです！
English: ...your alibi for the night of the murder no longer holds water!

Japanese: うむぐふうほわはああああァァッ！
English: Gwagwagwagwawaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 何か、言うことは‥‥？
English: ...Witness. Do you have anything to say for yourself?

Japanese: む‥‥むむ‥‥むむむ‥‥むむむむ
English: Grr... Mmm... Nnn...

Japanese: （‥‥よし！　これで、解決だ！）
English: (Alright! That did it! He's broken!)

Japanese: なるほどくん。まだ、 早いみたいだよ‥‥ガッツポーズ。
English: Um, Nick, I think it's still a little early for a victory pose.
...Huh?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
どいつもコイツも‥‥ 信念ってヤツがねえ‥‥。
English: It's so sad... No one has any conviction these days...

Japanese: し‥‥信念、ですか‥‥？
English: C-Conviction... you say?

Japanese: きのう‥‥満場一致でコイツを 怪人だと、キメつけたばかりだぜ。
English: Yesterday, we all decided unanimously that this man was Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: それを、今日は殺人者呼ばわり。 ‥‥浮気がすぎるんじゃねえか？
English: And now, we're calling him a murderer... You don't think we're being a tad fickle?

Japanese: た、たしかに！
English: Th-That's a good point!

Japanese: いやいや！　そこで ナットクしないでください！
English: N-No way! Don't fall for that too, Your Honor!

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙が‥‥ 毒島　黒兵衛を殺害した？
English: You say that Luke Atmey was the one who killed Kane Bullard?

Japanese: じゃあ、聞こうじゃねえか。 ‥‥いったいその理由は、なんだ？
English: Then let me ask you this. Why would he do that?

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ まっこと、そのとおりですな。
English: Heh heh heh heh heh... An excellent point.

Japanese: 動機ですよ、動機。 ‥‥ムウビング・マシイン‥‥
English: Motive, Mr. Wright! Motive! Might you my merry murderous motive manifest?

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！　なんか、 元気を取り戻してるよ！
English: N-Nick! He's getting his second wind!

Japanese: ニセのアリバイを用意して、 その罪を天杉　優作に着せた‥‥
English: If he prepared an alibi and pinned his crime on Ron DeLite as you say...

Japanese: そこまでの計画犯罪ならば、 たしかに、強い動機が必要です！
English: ...he must have had a very strong motive for murder!

Japanese: ソイツを持ちあわせていたのは、 被告人‥‥天杉　優作のほうだぜ。
English: The only one with any motive we've seen is Ron DeLite.

Japanese: そうだよなァ！　探偵さんよォ！
English: Isn't that right, Detective!?

Japanese: さよう。名探偵の調査によっても、 そのワカモノの動機は‥‥明白！
English: Indeed. According to my own research, the boy's motive is clear!

Japanese: 動機のないところに、今回の 殺人事件は起こり得なかった！
English: Without a motive, it's nearly impossible to prove guilt in a murder case!

Japanese: “動機”について‥‥あなたの 知っているコトを証言するように！
English: Now then... Maybe you can enlighten us to what the defendant's motives were.

Japanese: ‥‥かたじけない‥‥ご老人。
English: Thank you, Sir Old-timer.

Japanese: （なんとしても、ウタガイの目を 優作くんに向けるつもりか‥‥）
English: (They're doing everything they can to make Ron look suspicious...)

Japanese: （こっちも情報が足りない！ チャンスかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (Despite our lack of hard information, this may be our only chance...)

Japanese: ～毒島殺害の動機～
English: -- Motive for Murder --

Japanese: この哀牙、被害者との接点は まったくありませぬぞ。
English: I, Luke Atmey, had no points of contact with the victim whatsoever.

Japanese: 毒島氏は、怪人を調べていて‥‥ その正体を、とりちがえたのです！
English: Kane Bullard decided to investigate Mask☆DeMasque and simply mistook who he was!

Japanese: 脅迫状は毒島氏が書いたもので、 天杉　優作に宛てられたもの。
English: It was Mr. Bullard who wrote the blackmail letter and sent it to Ron DeLite.

Japanese: 被害者は以前、天杉　優作に ウラ切られた恨みがあったのです。
English: And it was again Mr. Bullard who harbored a grudge against Mr. DeLite for his betrayal!

Japanese: ただ‥‥被告人自身も、自分を 仮面マスクだと思いこんでいた！
English: Mr. Bullard's mistake is quite excusable. The defendant truly believes he is Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: だから、彼は毒島氏を殺害した。 ‥‥まさに、ヒゲキですな。
English: That is why Mr. DeLite saw it fit to kill Kane Bullard. Truly a tragedy...

Japanese: 被害者の毒島氏が、 被告人を脅迫していた‥‥？
English: So the victim, Kane Bullard, blackmailed the defendant...?

Japanese: コイツが‥‥ヤツの部屋から 発見された脅迫状だ。
English: This is the blackmail letter found in the defendant's apartment.

Japanese: 筆跡鑑定の結果、ブスジマ本人が 書いたものとカクニンされたぜ。
English: A handwriting test confirms that Mr. Bullard was indeed the one who wrote the letter.

Japanese: （そ、そうなのか‥‥！）
English: (Wh-What!?)

Japanese: 証拠品《脅迫状》の データを書きなおした。
English: Blackmail Letter updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、成歩堂くん。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～毒島殺害の動機～
English: -- Motive for Murder --

Japanese: この哀牙、被害者との接点は まったくありませぬぞ。
English: I, Luke Atmey, had no points of contact with the victim whatsoever.

Japanese: 毒島氏は、怪人を調べていて‥‥ その正体を、とりちがえたのです！
English: Kane Bullard decided to investigate Mask☆DeMasque and simply mistook who he was!

Japanese: 脅迫状は毒島氏が書いたもので、 天杉　優作に宛てられたもの。
English: It was Mr. Bullard who wrote the blackmail letter and sent it to Ron DeLite.

Japanese: 被害者は以前、天杉　優作に ウラ切られた恨みがあったのです。
English: And it was again Mr. Bullard who harbored a grudge against Mr. DeLite for his betrayal!

Japanese: ただ‥‥被告人自身も、自分を 仮面マスクだと思いこんでいた！
English: Mr. Bullard's mistake is quite excusable. The defendant truly believes he is Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: だから、彼は毒島氏を殺害した。 ‥‥まさに、ヒゲキですな。
English: That is why Mr. DeLite saw it fit to kill Kane Bullard. Truly a tragedy...

Japanese: ブスジマ社長とアイガ探偵を、 ゴーインに結びつけるんだよね！
English: So you're going to try to connect Detective Atmey to Mr. Bullard, right?

Japanese: そ、そうだね。 ‥‥できれば、ゴーインじゃなく。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right. Hopefully it won't require surgery either...

Japanese: そうなると‥‥やっぱり話題は “キョーハク”だよねー。
English: In that case, you'll have to talk about the blackmail...

Japanese: たしかにね。
English: That's true.

Japanese: （脅迫状‥‥ホントに優作くんに 宛てられたものなのかな‥‥？）
English: (Was that blackmail letter really intended for Ron?)

Japanese: 情報が足りなかったら、とりあえず ガクガクゆさぶってみようよ！
English: Since we don't have enough information, you should press him for more testimony!

Japanese: （そして、ガツンとたたきつけて やるんだ。‥‥ムジュンを！）
English: (Maybe I'll be able to shake some contradictions loose that way!)

Japanese: だれが、それを証明して くれるんですか！
English: And who in the world is going to prove that!?

Japanese: オレたち‥‥だぜ。
English: I will.

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛、およびＫＢ警備と 星威岳　哀牙のあいだには‥‥
English: After doing a thorough background check of Detective Atmey and Kane Bullard...

Japanese: なんの接点も発見できなかった。 捜査本部の公式報告だぜ。
English: ...we were unable to find any links between them. It's all in the report.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmmm...

Japanese: “探偵”と“警備会社”ですから、 シゴト上のつながり、などは‥‥？
English: Perhaps they were connected through their work? They were both involved in security...

Japanese: ねえ。
English: Nope.

Japanese: ‥‥一蹴されました。
English: That was blunt.

Japanese: さよう‥‥動機があるのは 被告人のほう、なのですよ。
English: In any case, the only one with a motive was Mr. DeLite.

Japanese: とりちがえていた‥‥？
English: Mistook...?

Japanese: “はきちがえていた”と 言ってもよろしい。
English: That's right. "Mistook". From the Old Norse "mistaka", meaning, "to take in error".

Japanese: ‥‥いやいや、 そういうコトじゃありません。
English: ...Umm, that wasn't what I meant.

Japanese: いったい、ダレと とりちがえていたと言うのですか？
English: Just who did the victim mistake Mask☆DeMasque to be?

Japanese: 考えるまでもありますまい。 ‥‥天杉　優作です。
English: Why, the answer is obvious. ...Ron DeLite, of course.

Japanese: （‥‥イヤミなほどに 自信たっぷりだな‥‥）
English: (Boy, I'd like to wipe that smug look off this guy's face...)

Japanese: 被害者は、どうして優作くんが 怪人だと思ったのでしょう？
English: Why would the victim mistake the defendant for Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: 貴方もわかっている ハズではないですかな？
English: Don't you already know that?

Japanese: ズヴァリ！　この ニュウス・ペエパアですよ！
English: Zvarri! Take a look at this newspaper!

Japanese: ほお‥‥これが《エマノンの涙》 ですか。ウツクシイ宝石ですな。
English: Oh ho... That's the famous "Tear of Emanon." A magnificent jewel!

Japanese: ‥‥写真には、ウツクシイ探偵も 写っておりますな？　ご老人。
English: ...That photo shows a magnificent detective as well, does it not?

Japanese: そして‥‥ミニクイ警備員 ‥‥被告人の姿が‥‥！
English: Furthermore... It also shows an ugly guard... Namely the defendant...!

Japanese: ひ、被告人‥‥！
English: Th-The defendant...!

Japanese: この記事を読んだ被害者は、 ズヴァリ！
English: The victim clearly misread this article and... Zvarri!

Japanese: ‥‥カンちがいした、という ワケなのですよ。
English: He got the wrong impression.

Japanese: 怪人の正体は、 このミニクイ警備員だと！
English: The impression that this ugly security guard was in fact Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほどねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: めずらしく フツーのスイリだったね。
English: That was an unusually reasonable deduction.

Japanese: （ミョーなスイリを長々と 聞かされると、気がめいる）
English: (I can't stand to hear any more of his long-winded stories.)

Japanese: （ヘタにゆさぶるのは やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'll hold off on pressing him further for now...)

Japanese: ‥‥けっこうです。 証言をつづけてください。
English: ...That's fine. Please continue your testimony.

Japanese: あたしは好きだけどな。 ミョーなスイリを長々と聞くの。
English: Aww. I was looking forward to one of his long-winded stories!

Japanese: ‥‥この脅迫状ですか？
English: You mean this blackmail letter right here?

Japanese: 《５００万円を用意しろ》と 書かれていますな‥‥。
English: It says, "Bring $50,000"...

Japanese: 筆跡は、まちがいなく 被害者のモノなんですか？
English: And the handwriting is, without a doubt, the victim's?

Japanese: まちがいねえぜ。 鑑識課の報告だからな。
English: There's no mistake. We have an official report to prove it.

Japanese: しかし‥‥この脅迫状、 宛名が書いてありませんな。
English: But, I don't see an addressee on this letter anywhere.
(An addressee...?)

Japanese: ‥‥コイツは、天杉　優作の 部屋から発見された。
English: This letter was discovered in Ron DeLite's apartment.

Japanese: そして、ジッサイ‥‥指定の日時、 天杉は取り引き場所に現れている。
English: And Mr. DeLite did show up at the designated place and time.

Japanese: ‥‥宛名なんか、カンケイねえぜ。
English: The fact that there is no addressee is irrelevant.

Japanese: （ホントに そうなんだろうか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What's up, Nick?

Japanese: ふと、思ったんだよ。
English: I just had a thought.

Japanese: 『あの脅迫状が、優作くんに 宛てたものじゃなかったら‥‥』
English: What if that blackmail letter wasn't meant for Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: え‥‥。そんな証拠があるの？
English: Whoa... Do you have any evidence of that?

Japanese: （この脅迫状‥‥どこか、 不自然なトコロがある‥‥）
English: (For some reason, I just can't shake the feeling...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、そんな気が するんだよな‥‥）
English: (...that there's something not quite right about this blackmail letter...)

Japanese: よろしいですかな？ ‥‥みなみなさまがた。
English: Well, everyone? Are you quite satisfied?

Japanese: それは、どういうイミですか！
English: What do you mean by that!?

Japanese: 貴方もわかっている ハズではないですかな？
English: Oh my, I was certain you were already aware.

Japanese: ‥‥ズヴァリ！
English: ...Zvarri!

Japanese: ＫＢ警備って、いろんな会社の セキュリティの情報が集まるし‥‥
English: KB Security has a lot of security info on all sorts of companies...

Japanese: ボク、いちおう警備チームの 隊長だったから‥‥
English: And since I was a security team chief...

Japanese: 会社からデータを 盗んじゃったんです。
English: I stole data from the company.

Japanese: ‥‥ブスジマ社長は、 天杉　優作をユルしていなかった！
English: ...Kane Bullard had yet to forgive young Mr. DeLite!

Japanese: だから‥‥怪人として罪を重ねる のを知りながら、脅迫をッ！
English: Which is why he sent him that letter upon mistakenly believing he was the thief.

Japanese: ここに、ＫＢ警備の社長室から 発見されたファイルがある。
English: Here is a file that we discovered in Mr. Bullard's office.

Japanese: ‥‥脅迫によってカネを 受け取っていた証拠だぜ‥‥。
English: Evidence that Mr. Bullard was receiving money through blackmail...

Japanese: む、むむう‥‥。し、しかし！ ヘンではないですか！
English: Hmmm... H-However! Isn't this a bit odd!?

Japanese: 被告人はなぜ、ダマって おカネを払ったのでしょう？
English: Why did the defendant pay Mr. Bullard the money...

Japanese: 自分が怪人でもないのに‥‥
English: ...even though he wasn't actually Mask☆DeMasque...?

Japanese: そこには‥‥やや不幸な めぐり合わせがあったのですよ。
English: On that point, there was an unfortunate bit of chance.

Japanese: あの‥‥ホンキで、そんな主張が とおると思ってるんですか？
English: Um... Do you really think that story is going to hold up?

Japanese: ‥‥ナニも、これは我がスイリ というワケではないのですよ。
English: That assertion is not merely my own, I'm afraid.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ここに、天杉　優作の妻、 天杉　希華の調書がある。
English: I have here a memo from Ron DeLite's wife, Desirée DeLite.

Japanese: 『ユーサクくん、自分を怪人だと 思いこんでいるの！』
English: "Ronnie thinks he's Mask☆DeMasque!"

Japanese: 『カワイソウでしょ？ ユルしてあげてよ！』
English: "Don't you feel sorry for him? Please don't think too badly of him!"

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ‥‥どうだ？　まるほどう。
English: Ha...! Well, Mr. Trite?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥。 （彼女のモノマネか？　今の）
English: Ugh... Was he trying to act like Ms. DeLite just now?

Japanese: 思ったより似てたね。
English: I thought he was actually pretty convincing.

Japanese: しかし！　それはあなたの 想像にすぎない！
English: Now that's just your imagination talking!

Japanese: やあれ、弁護士殿！ “想像”‥‥ちがいますぞ！
English: Come now, Sir Lawyer! I'm afraid "imagination" isn't the right word for it!

Japanese: これは、スイリなのです。 メンミツなロジックの集合体‥‥
English: This is a deduction. The result of carefully applied reasoning...

Japanese: そのスイリとやらを、 聞かせていただけますかな？
English: Can we hear that careful reasoning for ourselves?

Japanese: ‥‥‥話すと長くなるので、 またのキカイに。
English: It's a long story, and better saved for another time.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ じゃあ、いいです。
English: Hmmm... Very well, another time.

Japanese: （“話すと長いコト”って、じつは 意外とそうでもないんだよな）
English: (Those so called "long stories" aren't usually all that long.)

Japanese: （ジッサイに話してみると）
English: (At least, not in my experience.)

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっといいですか、 哀牙探偵。
English: Mind if I ask you a few questions, Detective Atmey?

Japanese: ム‥‥。ちょ、“ちょっと” ならば‥‥モンダイないでしょう。
English: Well... If-If it's just a "few", I guess it's alright.

Japanese: この脅迫状。“天杉　優作に 宛てられたもの”と考えると‥‥、
English: When you said that this letter was "addressed to Ron DeLite"...

Japanese: １つ、大きなムジュンが 生まれるんですよ。
English: ...I couldn't help but notice one major contradiction.

Japanese: む‥‥ムジュンが‥‥？
English: C-Contradiction...?

Japanese: 存在自体がムジュンみたいな アンタが、よく言うぜ‥‥
English: I don't know where a walking contradiction like you gets off saying things like that...

Japanese: （どっちがだ！）
English: (You're one to talk!)

Japanese: こういうとき‥‥オトコは コブシで語るモンだ。
English: At times like these, men are made to express themselves with their fists.

Japanese: 見せてもらおうじゃねえか。 ‥‥アンタの、コブシを。
English: Why don't you show us what you've got there, junior?

Japanese: では！　提示して もらいましょう！
English: Indeed! Time to man up, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 脅迫状の内容とムジュンする コブシ‥‥証拠品を！
English: Show us the contradicting evidence in the content of the blackmail letter!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
そんなホロホロなパンチ、 オレには届かねえぜ‥‥
English: A little two-one punch like that wouldn't even faze me...

Japanese: ‥‥そういう場合は、“ホロホロ” ではなく“ヘロヘロ”のほうが‥‥
English: I think you mean "one-two" punch...

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトのあげアシをとる前に、 もっとキチンと考えなさい！
English: Try thinking things through a little more before finding fault with others!

Japanese: （きくなあ‥‥ 裁判長のコブシは‥‥）
English: (Ouch... I sure felt that punch, Your Honor...)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥脅迫状の文面を もう一度、読んでみよう‥‥）
English: (Anyway... Let's try reading the blackmail letter one more time...)

Japanese: （どこかに、おかしな点が あるはずだ‥‥）
English: (There's got to be something off about it somewhere...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 証言に戻ってもらいましょう。
English: Very well then, witness. Please proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥この新聞記事を よく見てください。
English: Take a good look at this newspaper clipping.

Japanese: 《エマノンの涙》‥‥ 盗まれた宝石の写真があります。
English: It contains a picture of the "Tear of Emanon", the stolen jewel.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それが どうかしましたか？
English: Wh-What about it?

Japanese: モンダイは、 この宝石の“色”です。
English: The problem is this jewel's color.

Japanese: “色”‥‥？ ‥‥ニガテな話題だぜ‥‥。
English: Color...? I'm not much for discussing color myself...

Japanese: 記事によれば、盗まれた宝石の 色は《青》‥‥です。
English: According to the clipping, the color of the stolen jewel was "blue".

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！　脅迫状には、 こう書かれている‥‥
English: However, in the blackmail letter, a totally different jewel is mentioned.

Japanese: 《先日手に入れた、赤ダイヤを 処分することだ》‥‥
English: "I'll take that red diamond you received the other day."
R-Red...!?

Japanese: つまり！
English: Which means!

Japanese: 脅迫状に書かれた 《赤ダイヤ》とは‥‥
English: The "red diamond" described in the blackmail letter...

Japanese: 仮面マスクの盗み出した 《エマノンの涙》ではなかった！
English: ...is not the "Tear of Emanon" that Mask☆DeMasque stole at all!

Japanese: じゃあ、何か、まるほどおッ！
English: And your point is, Mr. Trite!?

Japanese: この脅迫状は‥‥ベツの人間に 宛てて書かれた！
English: So you are trying to say that this blackmail letter was intended for someone else.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、 そう言うつもりなのかッ！
English: ...That is what you're trying to say!? Right, Trite?

Japanese: 言うつもりなのですかッ！
English: That is what you're trying to say!? Right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 言うつもりなの！　なるほどくん！
English: Well? That is what you're trying to say!? Right, Nick!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: 毒島社長が脅迫していたのは、 この人物だったのです！
English: This is who Kane Bullard was actually blackmailing!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ビックリしました。 弁護人、一流の冗談でしたか。
English: I'm impressed, Mr. Wright. That was a first-class joke.

Japanese: 我がモノサシではかるならば、 二流ですな。
English: I'm afraid I can only rate it second-class myself.

Japanese: オレに言わせれば、 流れてすらいねえ。
English: Actually I don't think it had any class at all.

Japanese: あたしは笑ってあげるよ。 ‥‥ちょっとなら。
English: I could laugh... if I tried.

Japanese: （だ‥‥ダメだ！ ここまできて‥‥）
English: (Thanks everyone, for your undying support... Especially you, Maya.)

Japanese: （逃がすワケには行かない！）
English: (Argh! OK, we've come this far, and I'm definitely not about to let him slide!)

Japanese: ほお‥‥その目、まだ 死んじゃいねえようだな‥‥
English: Hey... It looks like you've got some life in you yet...

Japanese: もちろん‥‥ あなたですよ、哀牙探偵！
English: Naturally... It was you, Detective Atmey!

Japanese: ‥‥な‥‥何か コンキョでもあるのですかな？
English: Do you have some sort of basis for that claim?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥仮面マスクの 事件には、すべて関わっている。
English: You have been personally involved in every single Mask☆DeMasque case.

Japanese: そして‥‥前回の事件では、 エモノを取り戻しました。
English: And in the last case, you "recovered" what was stolen...

Japanese: そして、その際、 あなたは“お礼”として‥‥
English: ...and received a "jewel"...

Japanese: ‥‥《宝石》を 受け取ったそうですね。
English: ...as your reward.

Japanese: ほ、宝石‥‥だと‥‥？
English: A-A jewel...?

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、その指に ぶらさがっている‥‥
English: Probably the one wrapped conspicuously around your finger.

Japanese: 赤いダイヤのリングのことだッ！
English: That red diamond ring!
Unn... Ggg...!

Japanese: 脅迫状に書かれていたのは、 その宝石のコトだった！
English: That is the diamond referred to in the letter!

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛に脅迫されて いたのは、あなただったのです！
English: Which means that Kane Bullard wrote that letter in order to blackmail YOU!

Japanese: ぎィゃあああッ！
English: Aaaaahhhhh!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！ ええと‥‥その！　静粛に‥‥
English: Order! Order in the court! Um, um... Order, I say...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥おイタが すぎたみたいだな、弁護士さんよ。
English: It seems... You've gone too far with your childish pranks, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: （うう‥‥ミョーな ハクリョクを感じるぞ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... I don't like the way he said that...)

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛が 星威岳　哀牙を脅迫していた。
English: Kane Bullard blackmailing Luke Atmey?

Japanese: アンタ‥‥ホンキで言ってるのか？
English: Are you for real?

Japanese: え、ええ。そうです。
English: Y-Yes. I am.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ もっとムネ張って！
English: Nick! Come on, stand up to him!

Japanese: じゃあ、答えてもらうぜ！ ‥‥脅迫状のコトだ。
English: Then answer me this!

Japanese: コイツには‥‥ こんなコトが書いてある。
English: The blackmail letter contains the following passage.

Japanese: 『キサマの“正体”を 公表されたくなければ』‥‥と！
English: "If you don't want your identity revealed to the world"...!

Japanese: 書いてありますな‥‥たしかに。
English: Yes, it certainly does.

Japanese: 毒島は、星威岳　哀牙の “正体”を公表しようとした！
English: Kane Bullard threatened to make Luke Atmey's "identity" public knowledge!

Japanese: 公表されては困る“正体”。 そいつはいったい、なんだ！
English: An "identity" he wanted to keep a secret. So just what was that identity!?

Japanese: （哀牙は、その脅迫を恐れて 毒島社長の口をふさいだ‥‥！）
English: (Atmey killed Kane Bullard because he was afraid of his secret becoming known...!)

Japanese: （そこまでして守りたかった、 哀牙の“正体”とは‥‥？）
English: (What was the "identity" he wanted to keep secret...?)

Japanese: ここが勝負ドコロだね‥‥ なるほどくん！
English: This is what it all comes down to, Nick!

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙が、殺人までして 守りたかった“正体”とは！
English: The "identity" that Luke Atmey wanted to so desperately keep secret was his identity as...

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙は‥‥ 名探偵だったのです！
English: Luke Atmey... was an Ace Detective!

Japanese: ご紹介いただき、光栄しごく。 我こそ名探偵・星威岳　哀牙ッ！
English: Thank you for the kind introduction. I am Luke Atmey, Ace Detective!

Japanese: 魑魅魍魎の跳梁跋扈する頻闇の帷が 帝都を包む宵、決闘の緞帳が開く！
English: One embodies the nefarious forces of darkness. The other the angelic cherubs of light!

Japanese: ‥‥読めないし、イミも よくわかりません。
English: ...I am completely lost. Someone get me a translator.

Japanese: というか、そんなコト、 ダレでも知っていますぞ！
English: *ahem* But anyway, everyone already knows you're an Ace Detective!

Japanese: いくらなんでも‥‥ 今のはないよ、なるほどくん。
English: Come on Nick... Are you even trying anymore?

Japanese: （‥‥じつは、ぼくもそう思う）
English: (Actually, I kind of wonder myself...)

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙は‥‥ 怪人☆仮面マスクだったのです！
English: Luke Atmey... was Mask☆DeMasque!
Wh-What?

Japanese: ‥‥こっちが “あ、あれ？”‥‥だぜ。
English: That's my line.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: Wh-What's that supposed to mean?

Japanese: ‥‥こっちが“ど、どういうこと ですか？”‥‥だぜ。
English: ...That's my line, too.

Japanese: いいか。アンタ、今までずっと こう主張していたんだぜ。
English: Listen up, you. This is what you've been saying up to this point in time.

Japanese: “証人は、殺人の罪を着せるため、 仮面マスクのフリをした”、と。
English: "The witness pretended to be Mask☆DeMasque in order to pin the murder on the defendant."

Japanese: それならば！　イノチ果てる瞬間 ‥‥こうつぶやいて、笑って死ね！
English: In which case! You'll want to insist, up to and beyond the day you die...

Japanese: 『それでもやっぱり、 仮面マスクは天杉　優作だぜ』
English: ...that Ron DeLite was in fact Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: カンタンに信念を曲げるのは カンシンしませんな。
English: I must say, this sudden change of heart doesn't reflect well on you.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　仮面マスクは 優作さんじゃなかったの？
English: Come on, Nick! Wasn't Mr. DeLite the one who was actually Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: ううう‥‥もう一度、 考えなおしてみるよ。
English: Oof... OK, I'll try thinking about this again.

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙は‥‥ 脅迫者だったのです！
English: Luke Atmey... was a blackmailer!

Japanese: オイオイ‥‥ アンタ、こう言ってたハズだ。
English: Hey now... Isn't that a little different from what you've been saying?

Japanese: 被害者の毒島が脅迫していたのは、 この、星威岳　哀牙だと。
English: You said that Kane Bullard was the one blackmailing Luke Atmey.

Japanese: 脅迫されていた哀牙が‥‥さらに ダレかを脅迫していたってのか？
English: Are you saying that Atmey was blackmailing someone else on top of that?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。さすがにちょっと、 ヘンじゃないかなあ‥‥。
English: Ugh... You have to admit, that does sound a little odd...

Japanese: ヘンじゃないよ。 ‥‥それしか考えられないんだ。
English: It's not odd. ...It's the only thing that makes any sense.

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛は、星威岳　哀牙を 脅迫していました。
English: Kane Bullard was blackmailing Luke Atmey.

Japanese: しかし！　天杉　優作もまた、 だれかに脅迫されていたのです！
English: But! Ron DeLite was also being blackmailed by a certain someone!

Japanese: 最初の脅迫状が届いたのは‥‥？
English: So did you start to receive blackmail letters starting after this incident...?

Japanese: ええ。《エマノンの涙》事件から 数日たったころでしたね。
English: Yes. Just a few days after the "Tear of Emanon" heist...

Japanese: それからです。ボクのところに、 計画書が届くようになったのは。
English: After that, I started getting the plans in the mail.

Japanese: だれか親切なヒトが、イカした 大作戦を考えてくれるんですよ！
English: I received plans from some very kind person! Incredibly detailed plans!

Japanese: ‥‥“イカした大作戦”‥‥
English: "Detailed plans"...

Japanese: すると‥‥やはり、怪人の正体は 天杉　優作だった、と‥‥？
English: In which case, that would mean that Ron DeLite was actually Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: それが弁護側の主張です。
English: That is what we are claiming.

Japanese: ‥‥計画を立て、自分は手を汚さず エモノだけ、取り上げる。
English: Someone else came up with the plans, and had Mr. DeLite steal his targets for him.

Japanese: アイガ探偵！　これが、 あなたのやったことなのです！
English: And that someone... was none other than Luke Atmey!

Japanese: ‥‥シッ！　ダマって！
English: SHHH! SILENCE!

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ どうやら、見えたようですな。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee... Now I see. It's all becoming clear.

Japanese: なな、なんですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What is?

Japanese: 貴方は‥‥小学校のころ、 通信簿でよく、こう書かれた。
English: When you were in grade school, you received the same report card every year.

Japanese: 『そそっかしく、早とちりが多い』 ‥‥ちがいますかな？
English: "Careless, with the tendency to jump to conclusions"... Am I wrong?

Japanese: どどどど、どうしてそれを‥‥ッ！
English: H-H-H-How did you...!?

Japanese: ‥‥この哀牙が、天杉　優作を 脅迫していた、ですと‥‥？
English: You say that I, Luke Atmey, was blackmailing Ron DeLite...?

Japanese: ならば、当然！　彼と仮面マスクの 関係を、我は知っていたコトに‥‥
English: In which case, I would naturally know all about his relation to Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: それは‥‥たしかに、 そうなります。
English: Well, yes.

Japanese: 天杉　優作のもとには、２回目の 犯行から、計画書が届いた。
English: Ron DeLite started receiving plans from his second crime onward, correct?

Japanese: つまり‥‥１回目の犯行の際、 我は怪盗の正体を知った‥‥。
English: Which means... I learned of his identity when he committed that first crime.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。そうでなければ、 脅迫できませんからな。
English: Good point... You certainly couldn't have blackmailed him otherwise.

Japanese: それなら‥‥アツくてニガい 証拠を見せてもらおうか。
English: In that case, let's see some hot, bitter evidence.

Japanese: １回目の事件で、哀牙がなぜ、 天杉を仮面マスクと見抜けたか？
English: During the first crime, how did Luke Atmey know that Ron DeLite was Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥ヤケになって 証拠を投げないでください。
English: Mr. Wright, please don't just throw out any piece of evidence you like.

Japanese: オトコがヤケになったとき投げる のは‥‥サジ、に決まってるぜ。
English: The only thing a man should throw when he's frustrated is a hissy fit.

Japanese: （むずかしく考える必要はない！）
English: (Come on, there's no need to overthink this!)

Japanese: （哀牙探偵が、どうやって 怪人の正体を知ったか‥‥）
English: (How did Luke Atmey find out who Mask☆DeMasque was...?)

Japanese: （‥‥そのコタエは、 目の前にあるはずだ！）
English: (That answer has got to be staring me right in the face!)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そのカオ。まだ、 あきらめていないようですな。
English: Hmm... Judging by your expression, it seems you haven't given up.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、天杉　優作に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ron DeLite...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
